stories.title: Create and manage projects
stories.description: As a Business Analyst, I want to create projects so I can organise separate requirements efforts.
acceptance_criteria: I can create a project with a name and optional description.
The project appears on the dashboard with its document count.
I can soft-delete a project to remove it from the dashboard.

stories.title: Upload requirement documents
stories.description: As a Business Analyst, I want to upload PDF, DOCX, or TXT documents so the system can extract requirements from them.
acceptance_criteria: I can drag-and-drop or use a file picker to upload documents.
Files over 20 MB are rejected with a clear error message.
Unsupported file types show an inline error.
Uploaded files appear in the document list with their name and size.

stories.title: Extract and review requirements
stories.description: As a Business Analyst, I want to trigger requirement extraction so I can see what the system understood from my documents.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking 'Extract Requirements' starts extraction and shows a loading state.
Extracted requirements are grouped by category with confidence badges.
I can see a source reference tooltip quoting the original document text.

stories.title: Review and resolve gap questions
stories.description: As a Business Analyst, I want to see what information is missing so I can resolve gaps before generating specs.
acceptance_criteria: Gap questions appear in the right panel grouped by severity.
I can mark a gap as resolved and provide a free-text answer.
Resolved answers are automatically included in subsequent spec generation.

stories.title: Generate and review all spec types
stories.description: As an Engineering Lead, I want to generate Functional Spec, Technical Spec, and User Stories so I have a complete implementation-ready document set.
acceptance_criteria: Clicking 'Generate Specs' sequentially produces all four spec types.
Each tab populates progressively without waiting for all specs.
A version badge shows when a spec has been regenerated.
I can edit specs inline and changes are auto-saved within 500 ms.

stories.title: Export specs as Markdown
stories.description: As a Business Analyst, I want to export generated specs as Markdown files so I can share them or commit them to a repository.
acceptance_criteria: Each spec tab has an Export Markdown button.
The downloaded file is named {project}-{spec-type}-v{n}.md.
Export All downloads a single combined Markdown file.
